page: Amazon Web Services c7i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance powered by Intel Xeon 8488C processors. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this server features 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The dedicated CPU cores operate at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz and are supported by a 105 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per physical core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multi-core compute and memory bandwidth tests. The c7i.metal-48xl achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU score, and PassMark extended instructions. The DDR5 memory architecture delivers top-tier read and write bandwidth for large, uncached blocks. Additionally, the processor's support for Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) accelerates specialized workloads, as evidenced by top-tier scores in software compilation, ray tracing, and cryptography tests.

Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal configuration eliminates virtualization overhead, maximizing the return on hardware resources for compute-heavy workloads. The high core density and advanced instruction sets make this server highly suitable for high-performance computing (HPC), large-scale software compilation, intensive cryptographic operations, Redis database deployments, and CPU-based large language model (LLM) inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server designed for high-performance workloads. It is powered by Intel Xeon 8488C processors using the x86_64 architecture, delivering 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The system includes 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It features no local storage or GPUs, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic operations, aided by hardware acceleration flags like AVX-512 and AMX. The c7i.metal-48xl is highly suited for compute-intensive tasks, including high-performance computing, software compilation, Redis deployments, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 192 vCPUs, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for high-performance computing and multi-core workloads.

tagline: A bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated vCPUs and high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by Intel Xeon 8488C processors with 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores.
Equipped with 384.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory.
Delivers 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth without local storage or GPUs.
Features hardware acceleration for AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX).
Achieves top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance.
Optimized for high-performance computing, software compilation, and Redis workloads.
